mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in industrial production signals improving real activity and operating leverage for listed firms, so higher INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO momentum does not produce a positive 20-day return Rank IC above 0.03 in walk-forward tests, or if top-quintile momentum returns are not above bottom-quintile returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals weakening household income and recession risk, causing investors to lower earnings expectations; when UNRATE rises materially, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value for 2012-11 was 7.7 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in payroll employment and retail sales confirms that income growth is translating into consumption demand, raising near-term revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and RSAFS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value for 2012-11 was 133852 thousand persons
fact:RSAFS vintage value for 2012-11 was 412403 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive PAYEMS and RSAFS signal has no incremental predictive power over either series alone, or if its 60-day forward hit rate is not above the unconditional positive-return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates remain near the zero lower bound and CPI inflation decelerates, real discount-rate pressure eases without an immediate tightening threat, supporting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.50 percent and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation falls below its prior 12-month rate
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value for 2012-11 was 0.16 percent
failure_condition: Reject if this low-rate disinflation regime is followed by 20-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean in walk-forward tests, or if the interaction coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A large upswing in housing starts signals stronger construction activity, household formation, and durable-goods demand, creating positive earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10 percent over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value for 2012-10 was 894 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if 6-month HOUST surges above the threshold do not precede above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the triggered average excess return is negative after transaction timing lags.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp payroll slowdown reduces expected wage income and signals weaker business confidence, so a negative PAYEMS growth shock predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 50000 jobs or turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value for 2012-11 was 133852 thousand persons
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS downside shocks are followed by nonnegative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of triggered observations, or if the event-study mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases discount rates and tightens financial conditions, lowering equity valuations; higher 3-month FEDFUNDS momentum predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value for 2012-11 was 0.16 percent
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month FEDFUNDS momentum is not associated with lower 60-day forward returns in walk-forward regressions, or if the slope is positive over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP marks broad demand weakness and deteriorating earnings fundamentals, so a negative GDPC1 quarterly growth crossing predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses below 0 percent
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value for 2012-07 was 13638.1 billions of chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth crossings are not followed by below-average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if triggered observations have a positive-return hit rate above the unconditional baseline.
